title: Dialogue and quotes drill
description: Pure dialogue passage — train quote+Shift+punctuation under speed
text: "What time is the meeting?" Sarah asked. "At three," David replied, "in the main conference room." She nodded. "Should I bring the report?" "Yes," he said, "and the projections too. They'll want to see both."
tips: Quotes appear in 80% of fiction and chat-style writing
Type the closing quote without thinking — muscle memory
Dialogue tags ("he said", "she asked") should flow without pause

character_tips.anna: Dialogue is the hardest punctuation pattern. Master it = master writing.
character_tips.maxim: Half of business writing is quotes and dialogue. Drill them mercilessly.
character_tips.knopych: TWO PEOPLE TALKING! 💬 Type fast, switch fast!
character_tips.klavochka: A conversation on paper, my treasure 💌
